featured.name: Modelence App Builder
featured.why_radar: Most AI app builders stop at the demo. Modelence ships with bundled auth, database management, and monitoring out of the box. Open source, production-focused. If you are tired of gluing services together after the prototype stage, this is worth a look.

featured.name: Stitch by Google
featured.tagline: Turn napkin sketches into production-ready UI in seconds.
featured.why_radar: Google entering design-to-code is a big signal. Stitch uses an agent called Hatter to handle multi-step design workflows. Transforms rough sketches or text descriptions into editable designs and production code. Free, includes App Store asset generation, and ships with native MCP export.

picks.name: Anima
picks.tagline: AI that turns designs into pixel-perfect frontend.
picks.why_radar: Unlike generic code generators, Anima respects your existing design system. Works from rough ideas through Figma files and generates frontend code that matches your brand guidelines. Design accuracy over speed.

picks.name: Liner Write
picks.why_radar: AI writing agent for business-critical content. Proposals, reports, business plans. Backed by Liner Deep Research with 95%+ accuracy on SimpleQA benchmarks. Tackles the hallucination problem head-on, which matters when the output goes to clients.

picks.name: Orchids 1.0
picks.tagline: Build any app using your existing AI subscriptions.
picks.why_radar: Instead of yet another platform subscription, Orchids lets you plug in your existing ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini accounts. Multi-provider orchestration layer. Practical if you already pay for three different AI services.

hn_top5.title: 1Password pricing increasing up to 33% in March
hn_top5.summary: 1Password is jacking up prices by a third in March, which is frankly outrageous for what amounts to encrypted storage with a nice interface. The 146 points and 209 comments suggest users are rightfully furious about yet another subscription service deciding to squeeze customers.

hn_top5.title: 1Password Raising Prices ~33%
hn_top5.summary: Another post about the same 1Password price hike, though this one got less traction. The fact that it's being posted multiple times shows just how annoyed people are about losing one of the last reasonably-priced password managers.

hn_top5.title: NIST Seeking Public Comment on AI Agent Security (Deadline: March 9, 2026)
hn_top5.summary: NIST wants public input on AI agent security by March 2026, which is refreshingly proactive for a government agency. At least someone in authority is thinking about the risks before AI agents start wreaking havoc on critical systems.

hn_top5.title: Ask HN: How are you controlling AI agents that take real actions?
hn_top5.summary: Someone's asking the sensible question of how to actually control AI agents that can take real actions in the world. The low score but decent comment count suggests this is an important topic that hasn't gained the attention it deserves.

hn_top5.title: Show HN: Pilo – open-source agentic web automation engine by Mozilla
hn_top5.summary: Mozilla has released an open-source web automation engine for AI agents, which is exactly the kind of thing we need more of. It's disappointing that such a potentially useful tool from a respected organisation is getting so little attention.
